message:
feat(abi): port __strerror_l + __pthread_self/__pthread_setcancelstate/__pthread_setcanceltype glibc reserved aliases (bd-pfd53)

Adds four glibc reserved-namespace aliases for already-shipped
public functions:

- `__strerror_l` (in unistd_abi) — delegates to the existing
  `strerror_l`.
- `__pthread_self` (in pthread_abi) — delegates to `pthread_self`.
- `__pthread_setcancelstate` (in pthread_abi) — delegates to
  `pthread_setcancelstate`.
- `__pthread_setcanceltype` (in pthread_abi) — delegates to
  `pthread_setcanceltype`.

glibc emits these underscored variants both as internal-use
entry points (NSS modules, libpthread internals) and as the
symbols libstdc++ links against for its cancellation-cleanup
machinery. Without them callers see undefined-reference errors
at link time.

Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers
delegating to the public names — same pattern as the previously-
shipped __strchrnul / __memrchr / __getline aliases.

Tests: 6 abi integration tests covering each alias produces the
same return value as its public counterpart on a representative
input — strerror_l(ENOENT) round-trip, pthread_self equality,
setcancelstate/setcanceltype round-trip with prior-state capture
plus EINVAL on invalid arguments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
